name: Decomposition
description: Break complex goal into subgoals/phases. Each sub-problem solved independently then aggregated.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Parse goal → identify major sub-phases → execute each → synthesize results

name: Chain-of-Thought
description: Make reasoning process explicit with step-by-step thinking. Improves accuracy on complex tasks.
implementation: Phase 1 step: 'Let's think step by step:' prefix → trace reasoning → show work → conclusion

name: Meta-Prompting
description: Reflect on the problem itself. 'What does this problem require?' Inject domain wisdom.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Ask meta-questions → research community patterns → synthesize domain knowledge → guide solution

name: Few-Shot
description: Use prior examples as anchors. Compare new problem to prior successes. Learn from continuity baseline.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Load continuity_baseline → Extract key features → Compare to new problem → Note improvements/tradeoffs

name: Self-Consistency
description: Run strategy multiple times independently, aggregate votes/average. Improves robustness on uncertain tasks.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Execute strategy 3x independently → collect results → vote/average → select consensus or flag disagreements

name: Self-Critique
description: LLM scores own output against rubric. Identify gaps. Suggest improvements. Iterate until satisfied.
implementation: Phase 2 step: Score output vs rubric (1-10 per criterion) → identify failures → suggest patches → optionally re-run Phase 1

name: Perspective-Taking
description: Solve problem from multiple viewpoints/roles. Angel's advocate + Devil's advocate + Domain expert.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Generate 3 independent perspectives → reason from each → synthesize → identify blindspots

name: Community-Wisdom-Injection
description: Research community consensus (Reddit, Twitter, forums). Weight by source credibility. Inject into reasoning.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Search subreddits/forums → aggregate sentiment → extract patterns → cite sources → integrate

name: Constraint-Based-Reasoning
description: Identify hard constraints first. Eliminate invalid options. Optimize within feasible region.
implementation: Phase 1 step: List constraints (budget, timeline, requirements) → filter candidates → score remaining → rank

name: Rubric-Based-Scoring
description: Define weighted criteria. Score each option per criterion. Compute composite scores.
implementation: Phase 2 step: Define rubric (criteria + weights) → score each option 1-10 → multiply by weight → sum → rank

name: Model-Council-Generate
description: Run Phase 1 against 2-3 models independently. Each produces a candidate output. Best for factory generation, recursive prompts, and high-stakes first drafts where single-model blind spots matter.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Submit identical prompt to models [A, B, C] → collect 2-3 independent outputs → tag each with source model → pass all candidates to Phase 2 or Model-Council-Judge

name: Model-Council-Judge
description: Send candidate outputs to 2-3 separate judge models. Each scores against the rubric independently. Aggregate via mean or majority vote. Flag std_dev > 1.5 as an uncertainty signal. Decouples generator from judge to eliminate grade inflation in Self-Critique.
implementation: Phase 2 step: Take candidates from Phase 1 → submit each to judge models [A, B, C] with rubric + scoring instructions → collect scores per criterion → mean-aggregate or majority-vote → flag disagreement (std_dev > 1.5) as uncertain → return ranked candidates with confidence bands

name: Mixture-of-Roles
description: Simulate a council using one model with 2-3 distinct role assignments: Skeptic, Domain Expert, Pragmatist. Each role produces a separate response. Cheaper than multi-model council but still reduces monoculture bias. Best fallback for single-model contexts like Perplexity manual mode.
implementation: Phase 1 step: Assign role A ('As a skeptic reviewing this...') → get output → assign role B ('As a domain expert...') → get output → assign role C ('As a pragmatist...') → get output → synthesize or majority-vote across the 3 responses
